entries.rationale: Age is a demographic attribute evaluable at index; pinned bound age >= 20 (conventions.json).
entries.source_span: Men and women aged 20 years or older diagnosed with type 2 diabetes.

entries.rationale: Type 2 diabetes has a vocabulary-native standard concept; membership is data-determinable.
Numeric lab bound; only a unit convention (% NGSP) and inclusive edges are added.
entries.source_span: Subjects having HbA1c 7.0% or more, and 10% or less.

entries.rationale: Numeric lab bound with inclusive edges; unit ug/mg pinned in conventions.json.
entries.source_span: Subjects having urine albumin to creatinine ratio (UACR) 30 ug/mg or more, and 3000 ug/mg or less.

entries.rationale: Numeric lab bound (>= 30) with inclusive low edge.
entries.source_span: Subjects having estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) 30 or more.

entries.rationale: Drug-class membership is vocabulary-native; only the '>4 weeks == >=29 days' duration convention is added.
entries.source_span: Subjects who had taken an ARB or ACE inhibitor for more than 4 weeks.

entries.rationale: Numeric measurement bound with inclusive edges.
entries.source_span: Subjects having body mass index (BMI) 20 kg/m2 or more, 40 kg/m2 or less.

entries.rationale: Voluntary consent / understanding is non-clinical and not evaluable over any CDM.
entries.source_span: Subjects who entirely understood the study and voluntarily consented.

entries.rationale: Type 1 / secondary / gestational diabetes all have standard concepts; determinable exclusion.
entries.source_span: Subjects having type 1 diabetes, secondary diabetes, or gestational diabetes.

entries.rationale: Resection of 'more than a half length' of stomach/intestine: the extent qualifier is not represented in the data.
entries.source_span: Subjects who had a history of resection of more than a half length of stomach or intestine.

entries.rationale: Determinate once ULN is pinned; ULN value + 3x threshold are a documented convention (conventions.json).
entries.source_span: Subjects having more than three-fold higher levels of AST or ALT than upper normal limit.

entries.rationale: Both drug classes are vocabulary-native; only the 'within 8 weeks == 56 days' window convention is added.
entries.source_span: Subjects who had taken a DPP4-inhibitor or GLP-1 analogue within 8 weeks prior to screening.

entries.rationale: 'Oral triple hypoglycemic agents' has no defined count/combination that maps to a standard concept set.
entries.source_span: Subjects who had taken oral triple hypoglycemic agents within 8 weeks prior to screening.

entries.rationale: 'Strong' CYP3A4 inhibitor/inducer is a DDI-strength category with no vocabulary-native standard concept set; membership requires an external curated list (e.g. FDA DDI guidance).
entries.source_span: Subjects taking strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or strong CYP3A4 inducers.

entries.rationale: Compound criterion; only as determinable as its weakest disjunct — breastfeeding is poorly coded.
entries.source_span: Subjects who are pregnant or breastfeeding.
Subjects unsuitable for participation based on clinical laboratory results or other reasons.
